spec_match.rationale: A pure simulation facade. It detects support via 'selectedContentElement' in HTMLSelectElement.prototype but falls back to updating normal HTML spans (#tb-color, #bc-color, #sum-color, etc.) manually via a custom JS update function.

interactive.rationale: A highly complete interactive product configurator (color, size, material) driving three synchronized preview rows (toolbar, breadcrumb, summary card) with total price calculation and simulated timestamps.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the user configurator experience enabled by multi-selectedcontent syncing.

edge_cases.rationale: Excellent handling of pricing additions, blank options, description swatches, and clean output logs.

references.rationale: Lists correct references and MDN/OpenUI specifications.

design_system.rationale: Superb responsive visual layout, distinct preview panels, and nice active outline treatments.

openQuestions.title: Implement native select with selectedcontent
openQuestions.detail: When 'selectedContentElement' in HTMLSelectElement.prototype is detected, the configurator should render native customizable selects and place <selectedcontent> elements inside the toolbar and breadcrumbs instead of simulating the layout via standard spans.

summary: An exceptional product configurator demo that simulates customizable selects beautifully via JS events, but lacks native implementation fallback.
